title: clermontia arborescens subsp. waihiae
intro: Clermontia arborescens subsp. waihiae, commonly known as the Kaimanawa tree or waihia, is a plant species native to New Zealand. It is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly in traditional Māori medicine, where it is used to treat various ailments, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues. The plant's leaves and bark contain compounds with anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, offering health benefits such as reduced inflammation, improved wound healing, and enhanced immune function. In herbal preparations, the plant's leaves and bark are often made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, providing a natural remedy for various health conditions.
